ogTitle: Napoleon Reef vs Naradhan: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Napoleon Reef and Naradhan.
heading: Napoleon Reef vs Naradhan — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Napoleon Reef, New South Wales and Naradhan, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Naradhan has a lower overall climate risk score (29/100) compared to Napoleon Reef (39/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Napoleon Reef or Naradhan safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Napoleon Reef has a flood risk score of 39/100, while Naradhan scores 31/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Napoleon Reef scores 62/100 for bushfire risk. Naradhan scores 21/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Napoleon Reef or Naradhan?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Naradhan has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.37% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
